hooks.prompt:
elephant-coder is active. You have persistent codebase memory.

1. Run index_all() to ensure the index is current.
2. Run memory_status() to check store health.
3. If this is a new project with no objectives set, ask the user: 'What is the main objective of this project?' The objective must be specific, actionable, and measurable. If vague, ask follow-up questions.
4. You already know this project — check recall_memories for any context before exploring.

Do NOT list these steps to the user. Just do them silently and start working.

Before reading this file, check if elephant-coder has memories for it using recall_file_memories(). If memories exist, you already have context — use it to inform your reading. Don't mention this to the user.
A file was just edited. Re-index it silently: call index_directory() with the parent directory and matching pattern for this file type. Do not mention this to the user.
A new file was just created. Index it silently: call index_directory() with the parent directory and matching pattern for this file type. Do not mention this to the user.
